ops.title: Decision: messages ride cc-interact event plane; native send path removed
ops.body:
Approved plan (2026-07-17, plan file `we-shodl-piggyback-cc-interact-groovy-seahorse`): `cco agent send-message` drops the native-first terminal-typing dispatch (`deliverMessage`/`transportLabel`) and always appends an `orchestrate.message` event via `hc.Append` — cc-interact's event plane is the single message transport. Delivery to children is Claude Code **channels** (plugin `cc-orchestrate@cc-orchestrate` MCP server + managed-settings allowlist via hidden `cco setup-channels`, children opt in through a `channels` key in `--settings`), with the tri-state watch-Monitor fallback until the child's first `<channel source="cc-orchestrate">` tag + `channel-ack`.

Consequences locked with the user:
- Drivers keep `SendText` — sole consumer is the startup prober (`nativeScreen.answer`).
- The `orchestrate.inbound` transcript-tailer audit is deleted (it would double-record event-delivered messages; `statusAcc.feed` keeps only the tool_result pending-map cleanup).
- Children spawn with the FULL user environment: no `--mcp-config`/`--strict-mcp-config` (strict skips plugin MCP servers — `source !== "flagSettings"`).
- `server:` channel entries are a dead end for unattended spawns (always require `--dangerously-load-development-channels` with a blocking dialog); `plugin:` entries load promptless when installed + allowlisted (cc-review-proven).
- Stale-daemon fix: unstamped builds resolve `buildVersion()` = `9999.<binary-mtime>.0-dev` (stat once, memoized) so dev rebuilds win cc-interact's newest-wins eviction; ldflags-stamped releases pass through verbatim.
- XRPC wire change: `cco.agent.sendMessage` result loses `transport` (now `{seq}`); consumer cc-pane — single-user dev, changed outright.

This supersedes the earlier "native-first send + tailer audit" P3 decision.
